t73.subject: Design the slash_commands! macro + SlashCommand descriptor
t73.description: In a new crates/jfc-ui/src/input/slash_commands/registry.rs (or top of slash_commands.rs), define: (1) a `struct SlashSpec { name: &str, aliases: &[&str], help: &str }` descriptor; (2) a `macro_rules! slash_commands!` that takes rows like `"/clear" [] "clear the conversation" => clear_handler` and emits BOTH (a) a `pub(crate) const SLASH_COMMANDS: &[SlashSpec]` static table for autocomplete/help, AND (b) the `match parts[0] { ... }` dispatch arms calling each async handler. Macro must live in the same module as use site per kpreid's guidance. Handler signature: async fn(app: &mut App, parts: &[&str], text: &str, tx: Option<&mpsc::Sender<AppEvent>>). Include a trailing fallthrough hook for the skill-name fallback. Do NOT yet move handler bodies — just establish the macro + descriptor shape and get it compiling with a couple of sample commands.

t113.subject: Add allowed_tools config system
t113.description:
Add a convenient `allowed_tools` shorthand to the existing PermissionAutomationConfig. Currently users must write verbose rules:

```toml
[permission_automation]
enabled = true
[[permission_automation.rules]]
action = "allow"
tool = "Read"
```

Add a top-level shorthand:
```toml
[permission_automation]
enabled = true
allowed_tools = ["Read", "Glob", "Grep", "WebSearch", "WebFetch"]
```

Implementation: add `allowed_tools: Vec<String>` field to PermissionAutomationConfig. In the RuleSet::from_config (permissions.rs), expand allowed_tools into synthetic Allow rules with tool=exact and path=* BEFORE the explicit rules list (so explicit rules can still override). Also add a corresponding `denied_tools` shorthand for symmetry (already partially exists as `disallowed_tools` on AgentConfig — unify the naming). Wire into existing evaluate() path in permissions.rs.

t123.subject: Auto-allow tools when sandboxed (landlock)
t123.description:
When the landlock-sandbox feature is enabled and the sandbox is active, auto-approve Bash tool calls without prompting the user. The logic: if execution is containerized, permission prompts for shell commands add friction without security value.

Implementation:
- In the permission check path (app/permissions.rs or wherever tool_needs_approval is evaluated), check if `cfg!(feature = "landlock-sandbox")` AND the sandbox is currently active
- If so, return PermissionDecision::Allow for Bash/Write/Edit tools
- Add a config toggle: `[permission_automation] auto_allow_if_sandboxed = true` (default true when feature enabled)
- Surface a toast on first auto-approval: "Auto-approved (sandboxed)"

Files: app/permissions.rs, config.rs (new field), possibly sandbox/mod.rs for is_sandbox_active() query.
t123.acceptance_criteria: cargo check --workspace passes; when landlock-sandbox feature active, bash tools auto-approve; config toggle respected

t125.subject: Add allowed_tools / denied_tools config shorthand
t125.description:
Add `allowed_tools: Vec<String>` and `denied_tools: Vec<String>` fields to PermissionAutomationConfig in config.rs. In the RuleSet construction (permissions.rs), expand these into synthetic Allow/Deny rules with tool=exact, path=* BEFORE explicit rules. This gives users a one-liner:

```toml
[permission_automation]
enabled = true
allowed_tools = ["Read", "Glob", "Grep", "WebSearch", "WebFetch"]
denied_tools = ["Bash"]
```

Files to touch:
- crates/jfc-ui/src/config.rs (add fields to PermissionAutomationConfig, ~5 lines)
- crates/jfc-ui/src/permissions.rs (expand in RuleSet::from_config, ~20 lines)
- Add a test in config.rs tests that round-trips the new fields

The existing `disallowed_tools` on AgentConfig is per-agent tool filtering (strips from catalog). This new `denied_tools` is permission-layer (blocks execution with error message). Different purposes, keep both.
t125.acceptance_criteria: cargo check --workspace passes; new test in config.rs parses allowed_tools/denied_tools; permissions::RuleSet evaluates allowed tool as Allow and denied tool as Deny

t85.subject: Fix claim 6: FILE_LOCKS unbounded growth

t130.subject: Wire Workflow into stream tool dispatch
t130.description: DONE. tool_dispatch.rs splits Workflow calls alongside Task calls and routes each to spawn_workflow(): resolves script from scriptPath>name>inline (registry::resolve for names), parses meta + validates determinism, gates via permissions::decide (Deny blocks), generates runId + session dir, persists inline scripts to <session>/workflows/<runId>/script.js, registers a BackgroundTask via TaskEvent::Started, returns {status:async_launched,taskId,runId,scriptPath} immediately + releases the AllComplete latch, then runs run_workflow in background. On completion emits TaskEvent::Completed/Failed; the build_task_notification (<task-notification> with result/usage/agent_count/cache_hits) rides the summary field so the standard background-completion path surfaces it to the model and resumes the loop. cargo check --workspace clean.

t122.description:

  pub struct FallbackTriggered {

      pub fallback_model: ModelId,
  }

- In the UI stream handler, catch FallbackTriggered and push a toast + update app.model display

t124.subject: PDF reference pattern for large files
t124.description:
When reading a PDF via the Read tool or @-mention attachment, if the file is large (>10 pages or >1MB), return a lightweight reference instead of full content:

```rust
struct PdfReference {
    path: String,
    page_count: usize,  // from `pdfinfo` or estimate: file_size / 102400
    file_size: u64,
    display_path: String,  // relative to cwd
}
```

Implementation:
1. In the file-reading path (tools/read.rs or attachments.rs), detect .pdf extension
2. Shell out to `pdfinfo <path>` to get page count (with fallback: ceil(file_size / 100KB))
3. If page_count > 10: return the reference as formatted text instead of full content
4. The model can then request specific pages via a follow-up Read call

This saves context tokens on large PDFs while still letting the model know the file exists and its metadata.
t124.acceptance_criteria: cargo check --workspace passes; PDF files >10 pages return a reference summary instead of full content; pdfinfo failure falls back to size estimation

t54.subject: Fix broken test build: double-nested mod tests bug

Fix by removing the outer `mod tests { ... }` wrapper from the file-based test modules so the content lives at file scope with `use super::*;` directly.

t78.subject: Point autocomplete + /help at the generated SLASH_COMMANDS table
t78.description: Kill the second source of truth. render/overlays.rs:189 currently hand-maintains its own `const SLASH_COMMANDS: &[(&str,&str)]` (~21 of 88 commands, comment literally says "Keep in sync with handlers in input.rs"). Remove it and have slash_matches() / slash_popup() read the macro-generated table from input::slash_commands (re-export as needed). Also update the /help handler (from t75) to ITERATE the generated table instead of a hand-written list, so help can never drift either. This is the core payoff per kpreid: "a macro that generates a static table AND a function guaranteed to take action on all of the items in that list."
t78.acceptance_criteria: render/overlays.rs no longer defines SLASH_COMMANDS; slash_matches reads the generated table; /help iterates the table; autocomplete popup shows all 88 commands; cargo build compiles

t108.subject: Fix zero-tools continuation bug (stream stall root cause)
t108.description:
Already implemented. The fix has two parts:

1. `conversation_is_mid_tool_loop` (request.rs:54-60) — detects trailing ToolResult blocks in the last message, returns true so `action_expected` stays true and tools are NOT stripped.

2. `last_user_text` (request.rs:24-41) — correctly skips user messages that contain only ToolResult (no plain text), but this no longer matters because `mid_tool_loop` short-circuits before the heuristic is consulted.

Tests: 5 passing tests cover both the mid-loop detection and the action-intent heuristic. cargo check --workspace passes.
t108.acceptance_criteria: cargo check --workspace passes; post-tool continuation keeps advertised_tool_count > 0 when tools were available before

t74.subject: Extract handler bodies from slash_commands.rs into async fns
t74.description: Convert each inline match-arm body from slash_commands.rs (21 commands: /rename, /clear, /check, /compact, /advisor, /config, /continue+/c, /resume, /sessions, /workflow+/wf, /login, /logout, /release-notes+/releasenotes+/changelog, /feedback, /upgrade, /copy, /fork) into a free `async fn cmd_<name>(app: &mut App, parts: &[&str], text: &str, tx: Option<&mpsc::Sender<AppEvent>>)`. Keep handler bodies BYTE-IDENTICAL — just hoist them. Per the wg-macros guidance, "Functions should always be preferred over macros" for the LOGIC; the macro only generates the wiring. Place these fns in a new slash_commands/handlers_core.rs module.

t72.subject: Replace ext/ext2 slop with macro-driven slash-command registry
t72.description: Collapse input/slash_commands{,_ext,_ext2}.rs (3 files, 88 commands, ~2500 lines) into a single macro_rules! registry that is the SOLE source of truth for dispatch + autocomplete metadata. Grounded in the rust-lang Zulip wg-macros thread "Please help me figure out these very old macro guidelines": functions are preferred for handler LOGIC; a macro is the blessed tool when it "generates a static table and a function guaranteed to take action on all items in that list, significantly reducing human error" (kpreid). Macro defined in the SAME module as its use site. Eliminates the drifted second table in render/overlays.rs.
t72.acceptance_criteria: cargo build -p jfc-ui succeeds; cargo test -p jfc-ui passes; ripgrep finds no slash_commands_ext references; render/overlays.rs no longer defines its own SLASH_COMMANDS const

t127.subject: Add "ultrawork" keyword trigger
t127.description:
Detect "ultrawork" in user input (same pattern as existing ultrathink/ultraplan/ultrareview detection). When found:
1. Strip the keyword from displayed input
2. Inject a system-reminder: "The user included the keyword \"ultrawork\", which means you should use the Workflow tool to fulfill their request."
3. Log event

Look at how ultrathink is already handled (likely in stream/request.rs or input/submit.rs — grep for "ultrathink" to find the pattern). Mirror it for "ultrawork".

This is a prerequisite for the workflow system but works standalone — the reminder tells the model to use Workflow even before we have a full workflow runtime, since the model can still use the existing Task/Agent tools for multi-agent orchestration.
t127.acceptance_criteria: cargo check --workspace passes; grep finds ultrawork handling mirroring ultrathink; system-reminder injected when keyword detected

t121.subject: Workflow tool foundation (script runner + agent primitive)
t121.description:
Phase 1 complete. Implemented the Workflow tool foundation:

1. **ToolKind::Workflow** added to jfc-core with from_name aliases ["workflow", "run_workflow"]
2. **ToolInput::Workflow** with script/name/scriptPath/args/resumeFromRunId fields
3. **ToolDef** registered in defs.rs with full schema
4. **Dispatch arm** in dispatch.rs (returns "must be dispatched via streaming executor" like Task)
5. **workflows/ module directory** replacing the old workflows.rs:
   - `legacy.rs` — existing TOML step templates (backward compat)
   - `meta.rs` — JS meta block parser + determinism validator (rejects Date.now/Math.random)
   - `journal.rs` — JSONL append-only writer + reader with SHA-256 chain hashing for resume
   - `runner.rs` — WorkflowRuntime with tokio::Semaphore (min(16,cpus-2)), 1000-agent cap, key chaining
   - `task.rs` — WorkflowTaskState, WorkflowRunStatus, AgentProgress, generate_run_id
   - `engine.rs` — Placeholder for boa_engine integration (Phase 2)

16 tests pass. `cargo check --workspace` clean. Phase 2 (boa_engine JS execution, pipeline()/parallel() host functions, /workflows UI expansion) is follow-up work.

t106.subject: Ensure denied tools do not halt the agent loop

3. stream_done.rs:493-520 — EndTurn/other stop_reason + should_continue_loop → continue_agentic_loop

cargo check --workspace passes clean.
